title: rubus praetermissus
intro: Rubus praetermissus, a lesser-known species of the Rubus genus, is a type of wild raspberry native to the UK and Ireland. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits associated with Rubus praetermissus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Rubus praetermissus can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions with other medications.
